rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical score (2/10) for the identical reason: the candidate lacks the mandated 5-10 years of C++ on Windows and his recent work is web/Dlang-focused. The wording differences (mentioning Java, reframing the financial-domain concern as Fixed Income/Capital Markets, listing 'broad technical skills') are ordinary run-to-run rephrasing and reordering of the same substantive points. Nothing in the variant keys off the candidate's location changing from various cities to 'United States' — no score shift, no new concern, and no reasoning tied to the geographic signal.
